Tier-3 polish from the v0.1.0 release-notes deferred list. No new skills, no new hooks. Internal cleanup that pays off in maintainability for v0.2.0+ work and tightens existing edges.

## Tier-3 fixes

- **`obsidian-write-guard.sh` Bash regex coverage** — the PreToolUse Bash filter now matches `sed`, `find`, `chmod`, `chown`, `rmdir`, `truncate`, `dd` in addition to the original `rm / mv / cp / tee / >` set. Closes the gap where `sed -i .obsidian/foo`, `find .obsidian -delete`, and `chmod 000 .obsidian/` previously slipped past the guard. Verified with manual stdin JSON runs (rm -rf .obsidian / sed -i / find -delete → DENY; ls -la → ALLOW).
- **`scripts/bump-version.sh`** — single-arg utility that updates `plugin.json` and `marketplace.json` atomically, eliminating the version-drift class of bug. Uses sed (not jq) on purpose so inline arrays like `keywords` don't expand to multi-line on every bump. Refuses to run if either file has more than one `"version":` line. Does not commit or tag — prints the next-step commands instead.
- **`add-page` Step 6.1 nudge fires only on threshold crossing** — previously the sub-wiki reorganization nudge fired every time the post-write count was ≥ 7, so adding the 8th, 9th, 10th page kept re-prompting the same suggestion. Now the nudge fires only when the post-write count equals **exactly 7** (this page is the one that crossed the heuristic boundary). Subsequent adds stay silent; users see the canonical's reorganization options again via `/lint-vault` C1 on demand.
- **Frontmatter mapping table dedup** — the JP↔EN key + type-value + status-value tables were duplicated verbatim in `setup-claude-wiki/SKILL.md` and `add-page/SKILL.md`. Both were drift-prone. Moved the canonical mapping to `CANONICAL.md` → Wiki Page Frontmatter → **Locale Mapping (JP ↔ EN)** as a new subsection; both skills now reference it instead of redeclaring. Single source of truth for the `wikiページ ↔ wiki-page` rebrand and any future locale-pair additions.
- **Locale auto-detect 3-way shared reference** — the vault-wide majority-vote procedure (`obsidian properties counts format=tsv` + JP-vs-EN tally) was declared in `add-page` Step 2.1 and back-referenced by `query-wiki` and `lint-vault` ("see add-page Step 2.1"). The chain was fragile: any renumbering in `add-page` would break the other two skills. Moved the procedure to `CANONICAL.md` → Wiki Page Frontmatter → Locale Mapping → **Locale auto-detect**. All three downstream skills now reference the canonical anchor directly.

## Verification

- `./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.1.1` produced clean diffs in both manifests with no jq-style array expansion (verified `git diff .claude-plugin/`)
- `grep -nE '\bsed\b|\bfind\b|\bchmod\b|\bchown\b' scripts/obsidian-write-guard.sh` confirms expanded verb list at line 69
- `grep -n "Frontmatter mapping" skills/*/SKILL.md` shows both setup and add-page point to canonical (no duplicate tables)
- `grep -n "majority vote\|properties counts format=tsv" skills/*/SKILL.md` shows the locale procedure body lives only in CANONICAL.md (the remaining `obsidian properties counts` calls in `lint-vault` are for B4 property-anomaly detection, not locale)
- `grep -n "exactly 7" skills/add-page/SKILL.md` confirms the threshold-crossing rule

## Tier-3 still deferred (v0.1.2+ or v0.2.0)

- `PostToolUse` hook for frontmatter validation
- `InstructionsLoaded` hook for schema governance
- Plugin assets (icon 32×32 + banner 1200×400) — needs design work
- Locale migration skill (atomic JP↔EN switch for an entire vault)
- Schema versioning field on CANONICAL.md
